{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|zh|方墳}} | name = Tangean Kofun | native_name =丹花庵古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Shimane Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tangean Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|28|57.7|N|132|59|41.6|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Matsue, Shimane]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'in region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|28|57.7|N|132|59|41.6|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Tangean Kofun'''|丹花庵古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun| Kofun]], located in the Kososhicho neighborhood of the city of [[Matsue, Shimane]] in the [[San'in region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1933.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/163521|title=丹花庵古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tangean Kofun is a rectangular {{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}''|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} tumulus dating from the middle of the Kofun period. Although the mound has been considerably degraded to the extent that the lid of the stone sarcophagus is exposed, it measures approximately 47 meters on each side and 3.5 meters in height, and is built in two stages. Both ''[[haniwa]]'' and ''[[fukiishi]]''. The 2.08 meter lid stone of the sarcophagus is a slightly curved and has geometric patterns carved into it. Since it has been exposed for perhaps centuries, no [[grave good]]s remain, but fragments of a short armor and an iron sword have been unearthed. The tumulus is on private property with limited public access, and an explanatory placard once at the site was removed in 2018. The tumulus is approximately one kilometer to the east of from [[Asahigaoka Station (Shimane)]] on the [[Ichibata Electric Railway]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shimane)]]
==References== {{reflist}}

The {{nihongo|'''Tangean Kofun'''|丹花庵古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun| Kofun]], located in the Kososhicho neighborhood of the city of [[Matsue, Shimane]] in the [[San'in region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1933.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/163521|title=丹花庵古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tangean Kofun is a rectangular {{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}''|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} tumulus dating from the middle of the Kofun period. Although the mound has been considerably degraded to the extent that the lid of the stone sarcophagus is exposed, it measures approximately 47 meters on each side and 3.5 meters in height, and is built in two stages. Both ''[[haniwa]]'' and ''[[fukiishi]]''. The 2.08 meter lid stone of the sarcophagus is a slightly curved and has geometric patterns carved into it. Since it has been exposed for perhaps centuries, no [[grave good]]s remain, but fragments of a short armor and an iron sword have been unearthed. The tumulus is on private property with limited public access, and an explanatory placard once at the site was removed in 2018. The tumulus is approximately one kilometer to the east of from [[Asahigaoka Station (Shimane)]] on the [[Ichibata Electric Railway]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} | name = Tangean Kofun | native_name =丹花庵古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Shimane Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tangean Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|28|57.7|N|132|59|41.6|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Matsue, Shimane]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'in region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|28|57.7|N|132|59|41.6|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Tangean Kofun'''|丹花庵古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun| Kofun]], located in the Kososhicho neighborhood of the city of [[Matsue, Shimane]] in the [[San'in region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1933.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/163521|title=丹花庵古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tangean Kofun is a rectangular {{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} tumulus dating from the middle of the Kofun period. Although the mound has been considerably degraded to the extent that the lid of the stone sarcophagus is exposed, it measures approximately 47 meters on each side and 3.5 meters in height, and is built in two stages. Both ''[[haniwa]]'' and ''[[fukiishi]]''. The 2.08 meter lid stone of the sarcophagus is a slightly curved and has geometric patterns carved into it. Since it has been exposed for perhaps centuries, no [[grave good]]s remain, but fragments of a short armor and an iron sword have been unearthed. The tumulus is on private property with limited public access, and an explanatory placard once at the site was removed in 2018. The tumulus is approximately one kilometer to the east of from [[Asahigaoka Station (Shimane)]] on the [[Ichibata Electric Railway]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shimane)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://iseki.shimane-u.ac.jp/search_detail.php?target=phase&org_target=literature&subtarget=site&id=1413&search_kbn=site&page=1&sitename=西谷3 Archaeology Database of Shimane]{{in lang|ja}}

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} | name = Tangean Kofun | native_name =丹花庵古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Shimane Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tangean Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|28|57.7|N|132|59|41.6|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Matsue, Shimane]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'in region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|28|57.7|N|132|59|41.6|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Tangean Kofun'''|丹花庵古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun| Kofun]], located in the Kososhicho neighborhood of the city of [[Matsue, Shimane]] in the [[San'in region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1933.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/163521|title=丹花庵古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tangean Kofun is a rectangular {{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} tumulus dating from the middle of the Kofun period. Although the mound has been considerably degraded to the extent that the lid of the stone sarcophagus is exposed, it measures approximately 47 meters on each side and 3.5 meters in height, and is built in two stages. Both ''[[haniwa]]'' and ''[[fukiishi]]''. The 2.08 meter lid stone of the sarcophagus is a slightly curved and has geometric patterns carved into it. Since it has been exposed for perhaps centuries, no [[grave good]]s remain, but fragments of a short armor and an iron sword have been unearthed. The tumulus is on private property with limited public access, and an explanatory placard once at the site was removed in 2018. The tumulus is approximately one kilometer to the east of from [[Asahigaoka Station (Shimane)]] on the [[Ichibata Electric Railway]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shimane)]]

The {{nihongo|'''Tangean Kofun'''|丹花庵古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun| Kofun]], located in the Kososhicho neighborhood of the city of [[Matsue, Shimane]] in the [[San'in region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1933.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/163521|title=丹花庵古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tangean Kofun is a rectangular {{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} tumulus dating from the middle of the Kofun period. Although the mound has been considerably degraded to the extent that the lid of the stone sarcophagus is exposed, it measures approximately 47 meters on each side and 3.5 meters in height, and is built in two stages. Both ''[[haniwa]]'' and ''[[fukiishi]]''. The 2.08 meter lid stone of the sarcophagus is a slightly curved and has geometric patterns carved into it. Since it has been exposed for perhaps centuries, no [[grave good]]s remain, but fragments of a short armor and an iron sword have been unearthed. The tumulus is on private property with limited public access, and an explanatory placard once at the site was removed in 2018. The tumulus is approximately one kilometer to the east of from [[Asahigaoka Station (Shimane)]] on the [[Ichibata Electric Railway]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} | name = Tangean Kofun | native_name =丹花庵古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Shimane Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tangean Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|28|57.7|N|132|59|41.6|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Matsue, Shimane]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'in region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|28|57.7|N|132|59|41.6|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Tangean Kofun'''|丹花庵古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun| Kofun]], located in the Kososhicho neighborhood of the city of [[Matsue, Shimane]] in the [[San'in region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1933.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/163521|title=丹花庵古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tangean Kofun is a rectangular {{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} tumulus dating from the middle of the Kofun period. Although the mound has been considerably degraded to the extent that the lid of the stone sarcophagus is exposed, it measures approximately 47 meters on each side and 3.5 meters in height, and is built in two stages. Both ''[[haniwa]]'' and ''[[fukiishi]]''. The 2.08 meter lid stone of the sarcophagus is a slightly curved and has geometric patterns carved into it. Since it has been exposed for perhaps centuries, no [[grave good]]s remain, but fragments of a short armor and an iron sword have been unearthed. The tumulus is on private property with limited public access, and an explanatory placard once at the site was removed in 2018. The tumulus is approximately one kilometer to the east of from [[Asahigaoka Station (Shimane)]] on the [[Ichibata Electric Railway]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>

The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1933.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/163521|title=丹花庵古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>

Although the mound has been considerably degraded to the extent that the lid of the stone sarcophagus is exposed, it measures approximately 47 meters on each side and 3.5 meters in height, and is built in two stages. Both ''[[haniwa]]'' and ''[[fukiishi]]''. The 2.08 meter lid stone of the sarcophagus is a slightly curved and has geometric patterns carved into it. Since it has been exposed for perhaps centuries, no [[grave good]]s remain, but fragments of a short armor and an iron sword have been unearthed. The tumulus is on private property with limited public access, and an explanatory placard once at the site was removed in 2018. The tumulus is approximately one kilometer to the east of from [[Asahigaoka Station (Shimane)]] on the [[Ichibata Electric Railway]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} | name = Tangean Kofun | native_name =丹花庵古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Shimane Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tangean Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|28|57.7|N|132|59|41.6|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Matsue, Shimane]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'in region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = | Historic Site = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|28|57.7|N|132|59|41.6|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Tangean Kofun'''|丹花庵古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun| Kofun]], located in the Kososhicho neighborhood of the city of [[Matsue, Shimane]] in the [[San'in region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1933.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/163521|title=丹花庵古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tangean Kofun is a rectangular {{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} tumulus dating from the middle of the Kofun period. Although the mound has been considerably degraded to the extent that the lid of the stone sarcophagus is exposed, it measures approximately 47 meters on each side and 3.5 meters in height, and is built in two stages. Both ''[[haniwa]]'' and ''[[fukiishi]]''. The 2.08 meter lid stone of the sarcophagus is a slightly curved and has geometric patterns carved into it. Since it has been exposed for perhaps centuries, no [[grave good]]s remain, but fragments of a short armor and an iron sword have been unearthed. The tumulus is on private property with limited public access, and an explanatory placard once at the site was removed in 2018. The tumulus is approximately one kilometer to the east of from [[Asahigaoka Station (Shimane)]] on the [[Ichibata Electric Railway]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shimane)]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Tangean Kofun]][[en:Tangean Kofun]][[ja:丹花庵古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q55524706]] Wikidata name: Tangean Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:50, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Tangean Kofun | native_name =丹花庵古墳 | native_name_lang = | image =File:Tangean Kofun, funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Shimane Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tangean Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.482694|N|132.994889|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Matsue|Matsue]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:San'in region|San'in region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|28|57.7|N|132|59|41.6|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Tangean Kofun'''|丹花庵古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun| Kofun]], located in the Kososhicho neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Matsue|Matsue]] in the [[:en:San'in region|San'in region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1933.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/163521|title=丹花庵古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tangean Kofun is a rectangular {{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} tumulus dating from the middle of the Kofun period. Although the mound has been considerably degraded to the extent that the lid of the stone sarcophagus is exposed, it measures approximately 47 meters on each side and 3.5 meters in height, and is built in two stages. Both ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' and ''[[fukiishi]]''. The 2.08 meter lid stone of the sarcophagus is a slightly curved and has geometric patterns carved into it. Since it has been exposed for perhaps centuries, no [[:en:Grave good|Grave good]]s remain, but fragments of a short armor and an iron sword have been unearthed. The tumulus is on private property with limited public access, and an explanatory placard once at the site was removed in 2018. The tumulus is approximately one kilometer to the east of from [[:en:Asahigaoka Station (Shimane)|Asahigaoka Station (Shimane)]] on the [[:en:Ichibata Electric Railway|Ichibata Electric Railway]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Tangean Kofun]][[en:Tangean Kofun]][[ja:丹花庵古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q55524706]] Wikidata name: Tangean Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:50, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Tangean Kofun | native_name =丹花庵古墳 | native_name_lang = | image =File:Tangean Kofun, funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Shimane Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tangean Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.482694|N|132.994889|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Matsue|Matsue]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:San'in region|San'in region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|28|57.7|N|132|59|41.6|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Tangean Kofun'''|丹花庵古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun| Kofun]], located in the Kososhicho neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Matsue|Matsue]] in the [[:en:San'in region|San'in region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1933.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/163521|title=丹花庵古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tangean Kofun is a rectangular {{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} tumulus dating from the middle of the Kofun period. Although the mound has been considerably degraded to the extent that the lid of the stone sarcophagus is exposed, it measures approximately 47 meters on each side and 3.5 meters in height, and is built in two stages. Both ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' and ''[[fukiishi]]''. The 2.08 meter lid stone of the sarcophagus is a slightly curved and has geometric patterns carved into it. Since it has been exposed for perhaps centuries, no [[:en:Grave good|Grave good]]s remain, but fragments of a short armor and an iron sword have been unearthed. The tumulus is on private property with limited public access, and an explanatory placard once at the site was removed in 2018. The tumulus is approximately one kilometer to the east of from [[:en:Asahigaoka Station (Shimane)|Asahigaoka Station (Shimane)]] on the [[:en:Ichibata Electric Railway|Ichibata Electric Railway]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shimane)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://iseki.shimane-u.ac.jp/search_detail.php?target=phase&org_target=literature&subtarget=site&id=1413&search_kbn=site&page=1&sitename=西谷3 Archaeology Database of Shimane]{{in lang|ja}}

The {{nihongo|'''Tangean Kofun'''|丹花庵古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun| Kofun]], located in the Kososhicho neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Matsue|Matsue]] in the [[:en:San'in region|San'in region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1933.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/163521|title=丹花庵古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tangean Kofun is a rectangular {{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} tumulus dating from the middle of the Kofun period. Although the mound has been considerably degraded to the extent that the lid of the stone sarcophagus is exposed, it measures approximately 47 meters on each side and 3.5 meters in height, and is built in two stages. Both ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' and ''[[fukiishi]]''. The 2.08 meter lid stone of the sarcophagus is a slightly curved and has geometric patterns carved into it. Since it has been exposed for perhaps centuries, no [[:en:Grave good|Grave good]]s remain, but fragments of a short armor and an iron sword have been unearthed. The tumulus is on private property with limited public access, and an explanatory placard once at the site was removed in 2018. The tumulus is approximately one kilometer to the east of from [[:en:Asahigaoka Station (Shimane)|Asahigaoka Station (Shimane)]] on the [[:en:Ichibata Electric Railway|Ichibata Electric Railway]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shimane)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://iseki.shimane-u.ac.jp/search_detail.php?target=phase&org_target=literature&subtarget=site&id=1413&search_kbn=site&page=1&sitename=西谷3 Archaeology Database of Shimane]{{in lang|ja}}
Tangean Kofun
{{Kofun navbar}}
{{draft categories|
[[Category:History of Shimane Prefecture]] [[Category:Matsue, Shimane]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Square Kofun]]
}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Tangean Kofun]][[en:Tangean Kofun]][[ja:丹花庵古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q55524706]] Wikidata name: Tangean Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:50, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Tangean Kofun | native_name =丹花庵古墳 | native_name_lang = | image =File:Tangean Kofun, funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Shimane Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tangean Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.482694|N|132.994889|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Matsue|Matsue]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:San'in region|San'in region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|28|57.7|N|132|59|41.6|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Tangean Kofun'''|丹花庵古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun| Kofun]], located in the Kososhicho neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Matsue|Matsue]] in the [[:en:San'in region|San'in region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1933.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/163521|title=丹花庵古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tangean Kofun is a rectangular {{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} tumulus dating from the middle of the Kofun period. Although the mound has been considerably degraded to the extent that the lid of the stone sarcophagus is exposed, it measures approximately 47 meters on each side and 3.5 meters in height, and is built in two stages. Both ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' and ''[[fukiishi]]''. The 2.08 meter lid stone of the sarcophagus is a slightly curved and has geometric patterns carved into it. Since it has been exposed for perhaps centuries, no [[:en:Grave good|Grave good]]s remain, but fragments of a short armor and an iron sword have been unearthed. The tumulus is on private property with limited public access, and an explanatory placard once at the site was removed in 2018. The tumulus is approximately one kilometer to the east of from [[:en:Asahigaoka Station (Shimane)|Asahigaoka Station (Shimane)]] on the [[:en:Ichibata Electric Railway|Ichibata Electric Railway]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shimane)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://iseki.shimane-u.ac.jp/search_detail.php?target=phase&org_target=literature&subtarget=site&id=1413&search_kbn=site&page=1&sitename=西谷3 Archaeology Database of Shimane]{{in lang|ja}}
Tangean Kofun
{{Kofun navbar}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Tangean Kofun]][[en:Tangean Kofun]][[ja:丹花庵古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Tangean Kofun]][[en:Tangean Kofun]][[ja:丹花庵古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Tangean Kofun]][[en:Tangean Kofun]][[ja:丹花庵古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q55524706]] Wikidata name: Tangean Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:50, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Tangean Kofun | native_name =丹花庵古墳 | native_name_lang = | image =File:Tangean Kofun, funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Shimane Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tangean Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.482694|N|132.994889|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Matsue|Matsue]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:San'in region|San'in region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|28|57.7|N|132|59|41.6|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Tangean Kofun'''|丹花庵古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun| Kofun]], located in the Kososhicho neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Matsue|Matsue]] in the [[:en:San'in region|San'in region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1933.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/163521|title=丹花庵古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tangean Kofun is a rectangular {{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} tumulus dating from the middle of the Kofun period. Although the mound has been considerably degraded to the extent that the lid of the stone sarcophagus is exposed, it measures approximately 47 meters on each side and 3.5 meters in height, and is built in two stages. Both ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' and ''[[fukiishi]]''. The 2.08 meter lid stone of the sarcophagus is a slightly curved and has geometric patterns carved into it. Since it has been exposed for perhaps centuries, no [[:en:Grave good|Grave good]]s remain, but fragments of a short armor and an iron sword have been unearthed. The tumulus is on private property with limited public access, and an explanatory placard once at the site was removed in 2018. The tumulus is approximately one kilometer to the east of from [[:en:Asahigaoka Station (Shimane)|Asahigaoka Station (Shimane)]] on the [[:en:Ichibata Electric Railway|Ichibata Electric Railway]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shimane)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://iseki.shimane-u.ac.jp/search_detail.php?target=phase&org_target=literature&subtarget=site&id=1413&search_kbn=site&page=1&sitename=西谷3 Archaeology Database of Shimane]{{in lang|ja}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Tangean Kofun]][[en:Tangean Kofun]][[ja:丹花庵古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Tangean Kofun]][[en:Tangean Kofun]][[ja:丹花庵古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Tangean Kofun]][[en:Tangean Kofun]][[ja:丹花庵古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q55524706]] Wikidata name: Tangean Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:50, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Tangean Kofun | native_name =丹花庵古墳 | native_name_lang = | image =File:Tangean Kofun, funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Shimane Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tangean Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.482694|N|132.994889|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Matsue|Matsue]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:San'in region|San'in region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.5th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|28|57.7|N|132|59|41.6|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Tangean Kofun'''|丹花庵古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun| Kofun]], located in the Kososhicho neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Matsue|Matsue]] in the [[:en:San'in region|San'in region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1933.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/163521|title=丹花庵古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tangean Kofun is a rectangular {{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} tumulus dating from the middle of the Kofun period. Although the mound has been considerably degraded to the extent that the lid of the stone sarcophagus is exposed, it measures approximately 47 meters on each side and 3.5 meters in height, and is built in two stages. Both ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' and ''[[fukiishi]]''. The 2.08 meter lid stone of the sarcophagus is a slightly curved and has geometric patterns carved into it. Since it has been exposed for perhaps centuries, no [[:en:Grave good|Grave good]]s remain, but fragments of a short armor and an iron sword have been unearthed. The tumulus is on private property with limited public access, and an explanatory placard once at the site was removed in 2018. The tumulus is approximately one kilometer to the east of from [[:en:Asahigaoka Station (Shimane)|Asahigaoka Station (Shimane)]] on the [[:en:Ichibata Electric Railway|Ichibata Electric Railway]]. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shimane)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://iseki.shimane-u.ac.jp/search_detail.php?target=phase&org_target=literature&subtarget=site&id=1413&search_kbn=site&page=1&sitename=西谷3 Archaeology Database of Shimane]{{in lang|ja}}

The tumulus is approximately one kilometer to the east of from {{ill|Asahigaoka Station (Shimane)|en|Asahigaoka Station (Shimane)|WD=Q11517448|lt=Asahigaoka Station (Shimane)}} on the {{ill|Ichibata Electric Railway|en|Ichibata Electric Railway|WD=Q128426|lt=Ichibata Electric Railway}}. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>

The {{nihongo|'''Tangean Kofun'''|丹花庵古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun| Kofun]], located in the Kososhicho neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Matsue|en|Matsue|WD=Q207321|lt=Matsue}} in the {{ill|San'in region|en|San'in region|WD=Q398310|lt=San'in region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1933.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/163521|title=丹花庵古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tangean Kofun is a rectangular {{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} tumulus dating from the middle of the Kofun period. Although the mound has been considerably degraded to the extent that the lid of the stone sarcophagus is exposed, it measures approximately 47 meters on each side and 3.5 meters in height, and is built in two stages. Both ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}'' and ''[[fukiishi]]''. The 2.08 meter lid stone of the sarcophagus is a slightly curved and has geometric patterns carved into it. Since it has been exposed for perhaps centuries, no [[:en:Grave good|Grave good]]s remain, but fragments of a short armor and an iron sword have been unearthed. The tumulus is on private property with limited public access, and an explanatory placard once at the site was removed in 2018. The tumulus is approximately one kilometer to the east of from {{ill|Asahigaoka Station (Shimane)|en|Asahigaoka Station (Shimane)|WD=Q11517448|lt=Asahigaoka Station (Shimane)}} on the {{ill|Ichibata Electric Railway|en|Ichibata Electric Railway|WD=Q128426|lt=Ichibata Electric Railway}}. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>

The {{nihongo|'''Tangean Kofun'''|丹花庵古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun| Kofun]], located in the Kososhicho neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Matsue|en|Matsue|WD=Q207321|lt=Matsue|1=Matsue}} in the {{ill|San'in region|en|San'in region|WD=Q398310|lt=San'in region|1=San'in region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. ==Overview== The Tangean Kofun is a rectangular {{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}} tumulus dating from the middle of the Kofun period. Although the mound has been considerably degraded to the extent that the lid of the stone sarcophagus is exposed, it measures approximately 47 meters on each side and 3.5 meters in height, and is built in two stages. Both ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' and ''[[fukiishi]]''. The 2.08 meter lid stone of the sarcophagus is a slightly curved and has geometric patterns carved into it. Since it has been exposed for perhaps centuries, no [[:en:Grave good|Grave good]]s remain, but fragments of a short armor and an iron sword have been unearthed. The tumulus is on private property with limited public access, and an explanatory placard once at the site was removed in 2018. The tumulus is approximately one kilometer to the east of from {{ill|Asahigaoka Station (Shimane)|en|Asahigaoka Station (Shimane)|WD=Q11517448|lt=Asahigaoka Station (Shimane)|1=Asahigaoka Station (Shimane)}} on the {{ill|Ichibata Electric Railway|en|Ichibata Electric Railway|WD=Q128426|lt=Ichibata Electric Railway|1=Ichibata Electric Railway}}. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shimane)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
